sections.paragraph:
to lie with a woman, followed by an acc. Deu 28:30. (Arab. سجل pr. is to pour out water, to draw water with buckets, quod ad rem veneream translatum est, v. Diss. Lugdd. p. 168.)

Niphal, to be lain with, ravished (of a woman), Isa 13:16 Zec 14:2.

Pual, id. Jer 3:2. The Masorites have in every instance substituted for this verb, which they regarded as obscene, the שָׁכַב קרי. Hence-
